title: SyncBatchEnvelope
description: Request body for POST /api/ingest/sync-batch. One table per request, batch of rows. A single edge sync RUN may send multiple envelopes (one per table) sharing the same run_id.

contract_version.description: Semver of the contract the edge was built against.

node_id.description: Stable edge identity, e.g. 'rpi-lab-01'. Must be registered in edge_nodes.

db_epoch.description: UUID regenerated whenever the edge SQLite DB is (re)initialized/reflashed. Detects rowid reuse: if server sees a NEW db_epoch for a node, it resets cursors for that (node, table) to avoid silently overwriting unrelated historical rows.

run_id.description: UUID per single-run sync execution. Shared across all table envelopes in one run. Idempotency/trace key.

sequence.description: 1-based index of this table envelope within the run (table #1, #2, ...).

cursor.description: The cursor position the edge BELIEVES it is sending from. Server treats accepted_cursor in its response as authoritative; edge must store the server-returned cursor, not compute its own.

last_edge_id.description: For 'append' strategy: highest edge SQLite id already ACKed. Rows in this batch have id > last_edge_id.

last_change_seq.description: For 'summary' strategy: highest monotonic change_seq already ACKed. change_seq is bumped by an AFTER INSERT/UPDATE trigger on the edge summary tables, so late updates to old minutes always get a higher change_seq and are never missed.

rows.description: Row objects validated against the per-table schema in schema/rows/<table>.json. Each row carries edge_id (append) or change_seq (summary) so the server can compute accepted_cursor.
